title: Nawabi Foundation and British Influence
focus: Covers the establishment of the Nawabi of Awadh by Saadat Khan and the impact of the Battle of Buxar and Treaty of Allahabad.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 050]
The Birth of Awadh's Independence
1719-1722
Until 1719, Awadh was a province of the Mughal Empire administered by a governor appointed by the emperor.
Persian adventurer Saadat Khan, also known as Burhan-ul-Mulk, was appointed Nawab of Awadh in 1722 and established his court in Faizabad, near Lucknow.

[Page 051]
The Battle of Buxar and Its Aftermath
Battle of Buxar
Fought between East India Company and combined forces, leading to Awadh's defeat
Treaty of Allahabad
Signed in 1765, forcing Awadh under Company administration
British Control
Marked the beginning of British influence over Awadh's affairs
